entry.title: Report that Shows Purchase Orders that have attachments uploaded.
entry.content:
Hello,

Is there a report that shows purchase orders (approved, in-approval, etc. - preferable to include all statuses) that have at least one attachment? Perhaps there is a way to pull this through Monitor Purchase Orders or Manage Purchase Orders? Or a different application?

I am trying to easily identify what purchase orders have an attachment included without having to actually click and view every PO.

entry.title: Transitive attribute in SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
Preface

Transitive attribute is a useful feature in SAP BW, this blog will explore the possibility of implementing transitive attributes in SAP Datasphere

Disclaimer: All the data created are for demo purposes, the approach is used to demo the functionality of the currency conversion in SAP Datasphere, not intended for production design or usage.

Implementation:

Let's first draw the diagram to explain the concept of Transitive attribute, our goal is to display the attributes: Address and postcode from Company Code

Country Spend is transaction data, it has Country dimension, Company Code dimension and measure Amount.

Country dimension has attributes: Company Code, Language, and Company City

Company Code dimension has attributes: Address and Postcode

In SAP Datasphere, we will create the same data model according to the above diagram:

Company Code dimension with its attribute (no association, Company code as key):

Country dimension with its attribute ( Compound key: Country + Company Code, association to Company Code), Country dimension act as a bridge to navigate to Company Code

Association to Company Code dimension:

Country Spend transaction data with association only to Country dimension, not to Company dimension

Create an Analytical Model on top of Country Spend view

Important note: we enable the attribute Country only and can see the associated dimension: Country

If we click on associated dimension: Country, dimension Company Code with transitive attribute: Address and Postcode will show up

Now it is time to show the magic with Data Preview 😀:

The attribute of Company Code can display nicely in the final output.  

Summary:

The way to implement transitive attribute is similar to BW and functionality can be achieved perfectly in SAP Datasphere without much effort. 

Feel free to post any questions you may have, thank you for your time.

entry.title: Report(s) that show After the Fact (ATF) Purchase Orders
entry.content: I am looking for some guidance on how to run a report or set of reports that will show After-The-Fact (ATF) purchase orders. Specifically, I want to identify purchase orders that were created after the invoice was received. My initial thought was to compare Invoice date to purchase order date, as if the invoice date is before the PO date this would indicate an after the fact PO. However, it appears there is no way to easily connect or run a report that shows invoices with invoice date AND the corresponding PO number. Any help or guidance would be greatly appreciated.

entry.title: Re: Remote Code Analysis in ATC - One central check system for multiple systems on various releases
entry.content:
Hi @FAFR ,

yes his is correct. Alternatively you can use the Custom Code Migration app on SAP BTP ABAP environment.

Kind Regards,
Olga.

entry.title: Re: How to know the topic owner details in SAP Community...

entry.title: Predictive forecast tool in SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC)
entry.content: Hello experts, I am referencing the new standard SAP delivered model SAP__FI_CLM_IM_LIQUIDITY_PLAN for Liquidity planning stories, however I do not see the predictive forecast feature in the tool, can you please suggest the possible issue for this, on our previous model SAP__FI_CLM_IM_LIQPLN, we have the  predictive forecast option available.

entry.title: Re: 🟡 Petoberfest 2024

entry.title: Re: API_PURCHASECONTRACT_PROCESS_SRV how to update Contra...
entry.content:
Hi 

Please refer to this KBA on this subject -3338491
https://me.sap.com/notes/3338491
You can use a patch in a batch eg: 
Your URL --- */sap/opu/odata/SAP/API_PURCHASECONTRACT_PROCESS_SRV;v=0002/$batch
content-type multipart/mixed;boundary=batch

payload example: 

--batch
Content-Type: multipart/mixed; boundary=changeset

--changeset
Content-Type: application/http
Content-Transfer-Encoding: binary

PATCH A_PurContrItmCndnAmount(PurchaseContract='4600047031',PurchaseContractItem='00010',ConditionValidityEndDate=datetime'9999-12-31T00%3A00%3A00',ConditionRecord='0000653090',ConditionSequentialNumber='01') HTTP/1.1
Content-Type: application/json

{
"ConditionRateValue": "93.00"

}

--changeset--

--batch--

Kind regards
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: How to know the topic owner details in SAP Community...
entry.content: @kirankumar440 our experts all subscribe to tags and are informed like everyone else when new content is posted to one of those tags. No need to @ mention individual people and risk overloading their notifications.

entry.title: Re: Missing items during addition of ADT plugin to Eclipse
entry.content:
what eclipse package you downloaded? download this package

 

Eclipse IDE for Enterprise Java and Web Developers

entry.title: Re: Vertex accelerator
entry.content:
Hello,

Vertex Accelerator brings many tools that are important for the successful implementation of tax solutions with Vertex.  Many requirements which otherwise achieved via development can be done via customization now. For example: Tax data mapping allows mapping standard SAP table fields to Vertex mapping fields. This way additional data elements can be sent from SAP to Vertex for more accurate tax determination. Similarly, the tax code default tool brings tax code on AP transactions in SAP without the user selecting any tax codes. Vertex accelerator reduces custom development, reduces the need for training to share service team, and brings automation.

Thanks

Avnish

entry.title: Re: Adobe Document Services on SLES 15 SP6 non latin char...
entry.content: Nope, there is Adobe and Suse development involved. If you have simmilar case you can use 989575/2024 ticket as a reference.

entry.title: Shell Conversion - Remodeling of Aggregation Levels
entry.content:
Dear,

for a Shell Conversion I made the precheck of the BW-Objects with transaction RSB4HCONV (Transfer Cockpit). The BW where i made the check is a BW 7.5 on Hana.

One message of the precheck regarding the doings "before Transfer" is about aggregation levels.

There is a check message that says that the aggregation level needs to be remodelled. The reason for this is that the aggregation level has an infocube as its basis (in which navigation attributes are used). The note on remodelling https://me.sap.com/notes/2443001/E says that a multiprovider/composite provider must be built on the Infocube and the aggregation level must be built on this (before conversion). If a multiprovider/composite provider is built on the infocube and new aggregation levels are copied from the old ones, the new aggregation levels get new technical names.

Has anyone already had experience of how best to carry out this remodelling of aggregation levels? Because if there are new aggregation levels with new technical names, the queries based on the old aggregation levels will no longer work. This will also cause problems in the SAC, right?

Thanks,

Thomas

#BW4 Conversion, #Shell Conversion, #BW4HANA, #Aggregation Level

entry.title: Re: Setting Default Values for Create in ABAP RESTful application development
entry.content: I am trying out something similar. but not found any useful resource yet.

entry.title: Re: Stock Transfer
entry.content:
Hi 

Examples below: 
transfer posting from blocked stock to unrestricted via API 

Your URL */sap/opu/odata/sap/API_MATERIAL_DOCUMENT_SRV/A_MaterialDocumentHeader
content-type application/json

Example payload

{
  "PostingDate" : "2024-09-25T00:00:00",
  "GoodsMovementCode" : "04",
  "to_MaterialDocumentItem" : [
    {
      "Plant" : "1010",
      "StorageLocation" : "101B",
      "Material" : "SAP-TEST-X1",
      "GoodsMovementType" : "343",
      "QuantityInEntryUnit" : "1",
      "IssuingOrReceivingPlant" : "1010",
      "IssuingOrReceivingStorageLoc" : "101A"
    }
  ]
}  

Movement types and goods movement codes
1. GM_Code 01: Goods receipt for purchase order
2. GM_Code 02: Goods receipt for production order
3. GM_Code 03: Goods issue
4. GM_Code 04: Transfer posting
5. GM_Code 05: Other goods receipts
6. GM_Code 06: Reversal of goods movements
7. GM_Code 07: Subsequent adjustment to a subcontract order

321 Transfer posting stock in quality inspection - unrestricted-use stock
The quantity is transferred from stock in quality inspection into unrestricted-use stock.

323 Transfer posting storage location to storage location - stock in quality inspection
The quantity is transferred from stock in quality inspection in the issuing storage location into stock in quality inspection in the receiving storage location.

325 Transfer posting storage location to storage location - blocked stock
The quantity is transferred from blocked stock in the issuing storage location into blocked stock in the receiving storage location.

343 Transfer posting blocked stock - unrestricted-use stock
The quantity is transferred from blocked stock to unrestricted-use stock.

349 Transfer posting from blocked stock to stock in quality inspection
The quantity is transferred from blocked stock to stock in quality inspection.

Kind regards
SAP Support

entry.title: SAP role for BW4-Conversion
entry.content:
Dear all,

To carry out a BW4 conversion, there is a list with the required authorisation objects in the note https://me.sap.com/notes/2383530.

Is there already a standard SAP role that contains all these authorisations (which are listed in the note in the appendix under ‘Authorisation_Objects_List_Conversion’) or do you have to create a separate role for this and maintain all authorisations manually?

 

Thanks,

Thomas

#BW4Conversion, #BW4HANA, #Authorizations

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud ALM support for monitoring Content server
entry.content: I'm not sure if CALM has monitoring option for on premise MaxDB database. You should check it. For cloud environment only CMIS content servers are supported.

entry.title: Subscriptions: Sales Order without Customer
entry.content:
Scenario: We sell products that include the option to activate a subscription license after the sale. There is no additional price for the subscription, it is "free" to the customer.

Requirement: From an accounting perspective, we will assign a revenue amount to the subscription. But, we are thinking we will need two sales orders: (1) for the original sale to the customer and (2) a second order for the activation of the license.  The second order will not be able to have the customer as there is no further charge; but, we think we need it at the time of activation to create the RAR item.

Can we have a Sales Order without a customer?  Is there some other option we should be considering?

SAP Revenue Accounting and Reporting

entry.title: Re: In SAP Fieldglass, is it possible to autofill time sheets?
entry.content: Thanks for your question! To get you the best answer, we may need additional context of your business rules and process. Time can be autofilled via a time sheet upload/integration if you’re trying to eliminate a redundant time entry system or step. This assumes the hours are submitted in another system, then they can come over approved or pending approval. Time from the first day can also be copied to populate the remaining days on the timesheet itself by using a button that says “Copy values” on the left corner of the Day field. There is not a native config or rule that can auto-fill time sheets in the UI without an integration. Generally contingent labors payroll rules vary from full time employees, including variable work hours with unique payroll rules per location, multiple cost allocations may be needed, varying types of labor classification, etc. We would be happy to work with you directly to make sure the solution or guidance we offer is addressing your need. Please contact us via https://support.fieldglass.com/Fieldglass Thank you.

entry.title: Re: In SAP Fieldglass, is it possible to autofill time sh...
entry.content:
Thanks for your question! To get you the best answer, we may need additional context of your business rules and process.

Time can be autofilled via a time sheet upload/integration if you’re trying to eliminate a redundant time entry system or step.  This assumes the hours are submitted in another system, then they can come over approved or pending approval.

Time from the first day can also be copied to populate the remaining days on the timesheet itself by using a button that says “Copy values” on the left corner of the Day field.

There is not a native config or rule that can auto-fill time sheets in the UI without an integration. Generally contingent labors payroll rules vary from full time employees, including variable work hours with unique payroll rules per location, multiple cost allocations may be needed, varying types of labor classification, etc. 

We would be happy to work with you directly to make sure the solution or guidance we offer is addressing your need.  Please contact us via https://support.fieldglass.com/Fieldglass

Thank you.

entry.title: digital skills Initiative
entry.content: Hello @David_Chaviano  I am excited to participate in the Digital Skills Initiative, as I believe it aligns perfectly with my professional background and future aspirations. With over 2o years of experience in supply chain management, logistics, and leadership roles, including my current position at Boeing, I have developed a strong analytical mindset and adaptability to emerging technologies. I am eager to expand my digital skills to stay competitive in today’s evolving workforce. This program will enhance my ability to leverage technology in streamlining operations and decision-making, making me a valuable asset to any team. I look forward to learning and contributing to this initiative.

Is this indeed the case and if so can I please be provided with some config specs on said field?

entry.title: If a new item is added to a curricula, does it get reassigned to users to complete the item?
entry.content: We have a curricula which many users have already completed, but we want to add a new item to it and have everyone complete it. Will the system notify users that there is a new item they need to complete or does the system reassign the curricula even though it was previously completed?

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hi everyone! 

My name is Abubakar from Pakistan.  I studied electromechanics and worked as an electric engineer. But last year I got laid off and couldnt find a relevant job because the economy here is not very good. I started a small trading business to get some earning to support my family. Meanwhile I'm still actively looking for new career oppotunities.

A friend working in IT recommended me to try SAP career. I did my research for a while and found that I really liked how it works. Since I've been working for sales, I'd like to try out SD module or Sales Cloud to start with. I came across a post on Linkedin, where I learned about this initiative.  This will really help me with my financial situation and hopefully help me prove to future employers about my skills. 

Please kindly advise how to proceed to for the certification opportunity!  Thanks very much and glad to see so many people here with the same ambitions!

best wishes, 

Abu

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hello,

 
I'm Adnane Lahlal, I'm freshly graduated as logistics engineer and I did my internship for my final course project in SAP P2P, I've become really interested in the SAP field and see it as promising path to begin my careers as an SAP consultant within Logistics and Supply Chain.
 
Coming from Morocco, the SAP Digital Skills program has given me hope that earning an SAP certification could significantly improve my job prospects and career growth. I'm truly grateful for the opportunity SAP provides.

I've already started taking courses on the SAP learning platform, and I'm excited to continue exploring more learning paths. Below is a screenshot of the courses I've completed so far.

I hope to hear from you soon and offer my best regards

entry.title: Re: In SAP Fieldglass, is it possible to autofill time sh...

The situation is we are looking into implementing monthly time sheets with monthly rates for a select group, and my team wanted to know if these sheets could also be auto-populated so that the individual workers or suppliers don't need to populate the time sheets. In other words, so they would have default values and not require the user to enter hours/units for each day.

entry.title: Re: UI Annotation for masking field value
entry.content: We are on OData v4. The Smart Field is only for OData v2 as per the link you had shared.

entry.title: sap sac planning professional license in Prod
entry.content:
If I have dev, qa and prod SAC environments and we do planning apps, do I need planning professional license in QA and Prod since planning professional is the only one who can create planning models?  Do I need it in Prod for any other reason like locking data and copying/deleting versions.  If that can be done with Planning standard then I do not need professional license in prod.  My understanding is that I only need it in dev where the development work occurs.  And probably I do not need planning standard licenses in Dev since the developers will be having planning professional, correct?

 Dev- P Professional onlyQA - Plan Standard onlyProd - Plan Standard only
create planning modelsynn
do planning - enter datayyy
copy/delete/lock versionsyyy

Thanks

Abdul

entry.title: Local Development Issue Once Multi-tenancy Added
entry.content:
We are updating our applications to the latest versions of cds and cds-mtxs.  We have run into an issue that we did not seem to have with earlier version of cds-mtxs.  The issue is that once we run the command "cds add multitenancy --for production", the application no longer works locally using "cds watch".

I have recreated this issue in this public repo that has a stripped down CAP application with Fiori App.  Very little has been done on this repository.  It has a simple database schema/service projection with draft enabled.  It then has a Fiori Elements application associated with it.  I also ran the following commands against it as outlined by the CF Deployment guide in the official documentation:

 

cds add hana --for production
cds add xsuaa --for production
cds add mta

 

If you clone the main branch and run "cds watch", the application runs, you can navigate to the Fiori App, view the test data, and create new data if desired:

Running CAP serverGET from EntityRunning Fiori app

Now when we move to the next step of the deployment guide related to adding multi-tenancy, it has us run the following command:

 

cds add multitenancy --for production
npm install

 

If this is done to the repo linked earlier and "cds watch" is run, the application will no longer run locally.  Here, if you run a GET command against the lone Customers entity, you get the following error:

GET on CAP entity no longer working

As expected, the associated Fiori Elements app errors out in similar fashion:

Fiori App errors on initial loadFiori App errors on Create

To be clear, we are not trying to test multi-tenancy locally.  We want the application to run as a single-tenant application locally.  This has worked in the past when integrating "@sap/cds-mtxs", but this seems to no longer be the case.

When looking at cds.requires environment via "cds env requires" command, we see the following:

 

db: {
    impl: '@cap-js/sqlite',
    credentials: { url: 'db.sqlite' },
    kind: 'sqlite',
    schema_evolution: 'auto'
  }

 

We do not want a local "db.sqlite" database, but still want the database to run in memory locally.  To try to overcome this issue, the following was added this "development" specific config to the cds.requires section of our package.json:

 

  "cds": {
    "sql": {
      "native_hana_associations": false
    },
    "requires": {
      "[production]": {
        "db": "hana",
        "auth": "xsuaa",
        "multitenancy": true
      },
      "[development]": {
        "db": { "kind": "sqlite", "impl": "@cap-js/sqlite", "credentials": { "url": "memory" } },
        "multitenancy": false
      }
    },
    "profile": "with-mtx-sidecar"
  }

 

While this did force the database to load in memory, the issues we were witnessing persisted with the application.  What changes can I make to my application so that I can add multi-tenancy, but not run into the local dev issue I am seeing above?  All help on this would be greatly appreciated!

For reference, this is the "cds -v" output when running into the issue above:

 

-js/asyncapi: 1.0.2
@cap-js/cds-types: 0.6.5
@cap-js/db-service: 1.12.1
@cap-js/hana: 1.2.0
@cap-js/openapi: 1.0.5
@cap-js/sqlite: 1.7.3
@sap/cds: 8.2.3
@sap/cds-compiler: 5.2.0
@sap/cds-dk: 8.2.3
@sap/cds-dk (global): 8.2.3
@sap/cds-fiori: 1.2.7
@sap/cds-foss: 5.0.1
@sap/cds-mtxs: 2.1.0
@sap/eslint-plugin-cds: 3.0.5
Node.js: v18.20.3
cap-local-dev-issue: 1.0.0
home: /workspaces/cap-local-dev-issue/node_modules/@sap/cds

 

And here is my "npm ls" output:

 

cap-local-dev-issue@1.0.0 /workspaces/cap-local-dev-issue
├── -js/cds-types@0.6.5
├── -js/hana@1.2.0
├── -js/sqlite@1.7.3
├── /cds-dk@8.2.3
├── /cds-mtxs@2.1.0
├── /cds@8.2.3
├── /ux-specification@1.124.1
├── /xssec@4.2.4
└── express@4.21.0

entry.title: Re: 🔴 Automate your SAP Cloud Integration governance with CPILint
entry.content:
Hi Mohamed

This is not necessarily a good fit for static analysis. You often want to measure, right? That's, by definition, not static. That said, the Design Guidelines have some checks that are performance-related, so you could take a look at those.

Regards,

Morten

entry.title: sap.m.feedInput control error
entry.content:
Hi all, we are trying to use the sap.m.feedInput and we noticed this error on the console.

[DEPRECATED] The type 'sap.ui.core.TooltipBase' was accessed via globals. Defining types via globals is deprecated. In case the referenced type is an enum: require the module 'sap/ui/base/DataType' and call the static 'DataType.registerEnum' API. In case the referenced type is non-primitive, please note that only primitive types (and those derived from them) are supported for ManagedObject properties.

We are using the sap fiori 127.0 version

Any Inputs?

entry.title: Re: Disable or remove permissions for the Introductory Te...
entry.content: Our CSM verified that this ticket ECT-235689 is for the background image functionality, not the introductory text. SAP support researched further and has now stated that As per the further checking with the concern team please be informed you that there is no Pre-requisite mentioned in the documentation :Profile Preview on the Latest People Profile | SAP Help Portal , then this introductory text (self description ) will available by default in Latest People Profile and cannot be disabled. That is with Latest Profile the introductory text is available even if it's not enabled in 'Configure People Profile'. this is working expected. Disappointing and feel configuration needs to be modified.

entry.title: Re: Product showing out of stock, even though warehouse stock maintained
entry.content:
I have faced the same issue in Hybris 2211, I have added the stock [29 Qty ] for a product but in the website it's still showing " out of Stock" , i have checked I didn't added the price of the product so that i have facing that issue , after adding the price for the product my problem was solved and " Add to bag" button was visible and i am able to add the product in the cart.

This is OOTB functionality, To add the cart the product must have at least 1 price row [ amount can be 0 - No Issue]

This info may help..

entry.title: ZMM_MISSMATCH_PUR
entry.content:
Hello, 

I heard of this report, which may be a custom report, and I am trying to understand what inputs are needed to create it. I understand the output is a list of missing materials, purchased or made, needed for production/assemly. 

Does anyoen have an idea of what inputs are needed or how this report is created?

Thanks,

SAP Crystal Reports

entry.title: Re: Deploying a CAP based Fiori app to a central Launchpad
entry.content:
Hello,

Current CAP template generates a router inside the app folder, which I noticed that your application doesn't have. And it's said router that handles directing to the UI5 application or the server side of the CAP App.

Considering this, how would the steps you performed change?

entry.title: Fixed Pegging Relationship Exists for Material XXX. Please delete before execution - Msg no. SY499
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

Our client use PPDS, there are 2 Jobs which executes the "PRODUCTION PLANNING RUN". 1st JOB to UNFIX the pegging relationship and followed by the 2nd Job, which is used to FIX the pegging relationship.

But in past 1 Week, thrice the "FIX Pegging relationship" Job has got cancelled due to Error - "Fixed Pegging Relationship Exists for Material XXX. Please delete before execution - Msg no. SY499".

But the same Material is available in Variant of 1st UNFIX Job and it ran Successfully.

What should i do to fix this Error? Kindly help on the same.

Regards,

Jeffrey

entry.title: How do you mail paper check
entry.content: How do you mail paper check? do you use any external software?

entry.title: Confidently Global: How SAP’s customers navigate a dramatically changing globe
entry.content:
Welcome to the pulse of global business, where rapid change and relentless innovation set the pace. Whether you are a seasoned giant or an emerging start-up, navigating evolving regulations and adapting your business processes are essential steps for success. 

This blog opens a series of blogs featuring SAP customers that are successfully navigating a rapidly changing globe enabled by localized software solutions from SAP. Join us to learn from their experiences and gain practical insights that can help your business succeed in a dynamic global environment. 

Embracing a New Age of Taxation 

India's Goods and Services Tax (GST) showcases the power of digitization in driving significant economic change by streamlining tax compliance procedures, increasing transparency, and boosting revenue collection. These changes have contributed to a more unified and efficient economy with significant reductions in logistics costs. The Ministry of Road Transport and Highways reports that the removal of state checkpoints and integration of tax rates have resulted in a 20% reduction in logistics costs. There's also potential for further savings of 30-40%, making Indian manufacturing and consumer goods sectors more globally competitive. 

Similarly, the implementation of the Standard Audit File for Tax (SAF-T) is another example of how technology can enhance tax systems. SAF-T is an international standard for electronic exchange of reliable accounting data from companies to national tax authority. Its purpose is to simplify the administrative workload on enterprises by enabling more streamlined tax inspections and making tax information transfer more efficient. The growing adoption of SAF-T in nations like Austria, Luxembourg, Romania, Hungary, and Portugal is a testament to the global shift towards digitizing tax administration. 

Evolution in Digital Taxation Systems 

Furthermore, the digital transformation in tax systems has led many countries to take up e-invoicing and automate VAT returns. In this setup, tax authorities act as the bridge between suppliers and buyers, auto-filling tax returns with pertinent data from the transactions. Tax payers are then required to reconcile and sign. Some countries have also adopted the Global Anti-Base Erosion (GloBE) under Pillar 2 of the BEPS 2.0 initiative, which proposes a global minimum tax rate of 15% on the profits of multinationals. Others have adopted the Peppol standard for documents like invoices. This standard originated in the European Union but has since been extended by several Asia-Pacific countries like Japan and Australia. There are similar moves for global standards for handling plastic waste and other environmental initiatives. This is just a small list of changes being rolled out around the globe. 

The Future with Modern ERP  

In today's competitive global business environment, companies require more from their Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) systems than ever before. Modern ERP software has evolved into a dynamic platform offering extended capabilities. With built-in insights, automated processes, seamless workflows, standard integrations, and easy extensibility, businesses can now align their ERP systems to their strategic goals more effectively. Adopting modern ERP software with advanced capabilities is a game-changer for multinational companies operating in divergent markets. Automation of processes and workflows reduces manual interventions, thus minimizing errors and enhancing efficiency. The built-in insights can provide valuable foresight into local market trends, helping businesses gain a competitive edge. 

Standard integrations offered by these advanced systems make it more feasible to consolidate data from various local operations into a cohesive whole. This global visibility of operations enables compliance and empowers decision-makers to make informed strategic decisions. 

Go global confidently with SAP

In today's dynamic world where the only constant is change, SAP stands as a beacon of certainty enabling enterprises to go global with confidence. The trust that customers place in SAP stems from the deep understanding and strategic approach towards software localization. SAP’s software localization enables customers to derive value from their core business applications across geographies using standard processes.  

SAP customers generate 87% of total global commerce ($46 trillion). The vast majority of them trade across borders. Here are some examples of the customer complexities in evolving regulatory and operational challenges:  

  • A major player in industrial equipment has deployed SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition to support its service depots - this customer is live across 37 countries.  
  • One of the largest multinational professional services firms describes how SAP solutions are being implemented across their global network and how, as an SAP partner, they are helping customers with implementations and how their tax practice uses SAP solutions to supplement their own proprietary tools.   
  • Customers describe how SAP is helping them comply with FATOORAH, the e-invoicing imperative in Saudi Arabia and Angola’s SAF-T requirements and countless other document and reporting requirements around the globe.   

Looking to the future, SAP customers need to prepare for upcoming e-invoicing mandates across multiple jurisdictions, including Germany’s phased implementation starting January 1, 2025, under the ‘Growth Opportunities Act’; Poland’s National e-Invoicing System (KSeF) becoming mandatory in February 2026; and France’s nationwide e-invoicing and e-reporting mandate commencing in September 2026 for large enterprises. Fortunately, SAP delivers software updates to enable customers to comply with new and evolving regulations around the world. In combination with timely notifications about the relevant regulatory changes, this Regulatory Relief as a Service gives SAP customers peace of mind. 

SAP offers geographic coverage in more than 130 countries and providing more than 1000 local versions, which are adaptations of SAP’s software to meet specific local requirements, regulations, or practices, in over 40 languages. 

Summary 

As we rapidly navigate through a dynamically changing globe, businesses are leveraging technology to develop innovative solutions and thrive amidst challenges. From embracing new-age taxation and digital tax systems to enhancing ERP capabilities and ensuring efficient business operations, SAP stands confidently, propelling businesses towards global success with its comprehensive geographic coverage and commitment to software localization. As we embark on our upcoming blog series, we are excited to share some inspiring customer stories showcasing how businesses across the globe are leveraging the power of localization with SAP to thrive in our rapidly changing global economy. 

Stay tuned for upcoming localization blogs on SAP Community. 

Learn about Localization Today 

Localization Capabilities on sap.com 

SAP Localization Community 

Regulatory Change Manager: Your Solution to Navigate Regulatory Changes with Ease

entry.title: Re: Remote Code Analysis in ATC - One central check system for multiple systems on various releases
entry.content:
Hi @FAFR ,

Custom Code Migration app is free, only the SAP BTP ABAP environment must me entitled. See the blog for the setup https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/abap-custom-code-analysis-using-sap-business-technology-platform/ba-p/13401679 and see please this blog about custom code analysis for SAP S/4HANA with Custom Code Migration app: https://blogs.sap.com/2019/02/27/custom-code-analysis-for-sap-s4hana-with-sap-fiori-app-custom-code-migration/

Kind Regards,
Olga.

entry.title: Re: Deploying a CAP based Fiori app to a central Launchpad
entry.content:
Hi @lucas_a_m ,

please check out the beta feature cds add workzone at https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/guides/deployment/to-cf#add-ui.

entry.title: Re: Steps to navigate to a canvas/custom page in List Report application
entry.content: Even I see no UI loading. Any solutions on it

entry.title: Re: Mass Deletion of Contact Person - Customer Master Data
entry.content: Hi Joao Pereira, Unfortunately, I think there is no simple functional way to delete contact information from table KNVK. The only option is to write a small program using FM SD_CUSTOMER_MAINTAIN_ALL. Thanks Avnish

entry.title: Re: How to Properly Refresh a Page on Side Drawer Navigat...
entry.content: You can't close the page since it is the root page of the nav stack the close would be ignored. Clear history should always start back at the root but I am not sure if it reloads the page or not. I'll have to try it out when I have some time to expirement

entry.title: Cookbook IS-U/CCS: Billing & Invoicing (Installation Groups)
entry.content:
I refer to note 2578181 - How to find the Cookbook “Payment Scheme” where SAP attaches the Cookbook for an ISU topic.

We need to have a Cookbook, specifically the IS-U/CCS: Billing & Invoicing, which is mentioned as a reference in the Learning Hub IUT230 however, in the link that mentions the book (https://www.sap.com/industries/utilities.html) the indicated path is not found. We need specifically the section about "Installation Groups". 

Does somebody have access to this resource?

Thanks,

entry.title: Level Complete! SAP Inside Track Belgium 2024 Recap
entry.content:
Another fantastic SAP Inside Track Belgium is in the books! Last Saturday, we came together to share knowledge, connect with passionate SAP Community members, and celebrate everything that makes this community so special.

 

A Day of Learning and Connecting

The day was packed with insightful sessions from talented speakers who shared their expertise on a wide range of SAP topics. It’s always inspiring to see how much we can learn from each other and how strong the spirit of collaboration is within the SAP ecosystem. A huge thank you to all our speakers for their time and contributions.

 

Shoutout to Our Host

This year, we had the privilege of hosting the event at the ARHS Group's fantastic venue. Their support and hospitality truly elevated the experience for everyone involved, and we couldn’t have asked for a better partner to make SAP Inside Track Belgium 2024 a success!
 

 

A Pre-Event to Remember

Before the main event, a group of us had the chance to experience a brilliant pre-event Brussels City tour, expertly guided by Jeremy. While Belgium may be famous for its beer, Jeremy showed us there’s so much more to discover! From the stunning architecture to rich cultural spots, it was the perfect way to kick off the weekend. Thanks, Jeremy!

 

What’s Next?

As we wrap up another successful SAP Inside Track Belgium, we’re already looking ahead to the next SAP Community Meetup in Belgium. Stay tuned for more details—we can’t wait to do it all over again with you!

entry.title: Re: How to get URL parameters in SAP Screen Personas
entry.content: Can you tell me what version of Personas are you using in that screenshot?

entry.title: We Value Your Feedback: SAP Inside Track Belgium 2024
entry.content:
SAP Inside Track Belgium 2024 was an incredible day, and we’re so grateful for all of you who joined us! To ensure future events continue to meet the needs and expectations of our community, we asked for your feedback using Mentimeter—and the results are in!
 

What You Told Us

Here’s what we learned from your input:
 
- One or two tracks? The overwhelming majority of you prefer a single track to keep the focus streamlined and allow everyone to attend all sessions.
  

 

- Hands-on sessions or not? The answer was a resounding yes! Expect more hands-on, interactive opportunities in future events to dive deeper into SAP technologies.

 

  
- Full day or half day? Well, I admit, the half-day suggestion might have been a bit of a stretch. Everyone agreed a full day is the way to go for maximum value (lesson learned!).

 

- How many SAP Inside Tracks per year? Most of the community feels that one event per year is just right to balance time, content, and planning.

 

Help Us Choose the Next Location!

While we’re thrilled with the feedback, we still have one big question: Where should the next SAP Inside Track Belgium be held? We're considering several cities like Brussels, Antwerp, Ghent, or Liège, but we’d love to hear your thoughts! Is there another city you think would be perfect for the event? Let us know in the comments below.
 
Your voice continues to shape the future of SAP Inside Track Belgium. Thanks again for your feedback, and we can’t wait to see you at the next event!

entry.title: Re: SQLITE_CONSTRAINT: UNIQUE constraint failed error during CAP Tutorial Execution
entry.content: Have you already loaded the data once?  This looks to me like its saying the record with that unique key already exists.

entry.title: Identity and Access Management with Microsoft Entra, Part I: Managing access to SAP BTP
entry.content:
SAP and Microsoft have announced their collaboration to modernize identity and access management (IAM) for SAP customers. SAP recommends its SAP Identity Management (IDM) customers transition to Microsoft Entra (formerly known as Microsoft Azure Active Directory) as the SAP IDM solution approaches the end of maintenance by 2027, with extended maintenance until 2030. This blog post series aims to provide technical guidance on how to plan and implement IAM for SAP applications with Microsoft Entra following SAP's reference architecture for Cloud-leading identity lifecycle.

Scenario overview

This blog post series focuses on key scenarios, starting with the implementation of a workflow-based access management solution to SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) with Microsoft Entra ID Governance.

📢 📢 📢  Note
Don't miss SAP TechEd 2024 and check out session XP202, Identity lifecycle management with SAP BTP and Microsoft Entra, where @RegineSchimmer and I will focus on this scenario and show a live demo.

Figure 1 illustrates the scenario in more detail:

Figure 1: Access management to BTP with Entra ID Governance

The developer (John Doe) requires access to SAP Business Application Studio which serves as an example for an SAP BTP application in this scenario. To work with SAP Business Application Studio, John's user must be a assigned to the Developer role in the BTP subaccount, which is part of the Business_Application_Studio_Developer role collection that gets automatically created with the subscription to SAP Business Application Studio. Granting the access must be approved by the Entra ID administrator (Jane Smith).

To implement this workflow for managing the access to BTP, the following entities in Entra are required:

  • All users in the Entra tenant working with SAP Business Application Studio must be a member of the SAP BTP Developer Group. This is a group of type Security. 
  • The SAP BTP Developer Group is assigned to the SAP BTP Access Package. Access Packages are a feature of Entitlement Management in Microsoft Entra ID Governance that allows administrators to bundle resources such as groups, applications, and SharePoint Online sites into a single package. This package can then be assigned to users based on policies that define who can request access, approval workflows, and lifecycle settings. In this scenario, John requests his membership in the SAP BTP Developer Group by starting a workflow for the SAP BTP Access Package that Jane as an Administrator for the package can approve.

If John's request gets approved by Jane and he becomes a member of the Entra ID security group SAP BTP Developer, how is this information securely transferred to SAP BTP? Microsoft Entra ID issues security tokens to successfully authenticated users, such as access tokens, refresh tokens, and ID tokens. Depending on the token type they contain different information, expressed as name-value pairs (also known as claims), that reliably provide facts about the user's identity. Claims can include details like the user's unique ID, email address, the user's department, job title, and other attributes related to the user's role within the organization. To securely transfer John's membership in the SAP BTP Developer group to BTP, Entra can be configured to include John's group memberships as a claim in the ID token. Since different applications usually require different sets of claims in the tokens, customization of the claims is done on an application registration for IAS (SAP Cloud Identity Services Application Registration in figure 1) in the Entra tenant.

When John opens the URL to SAP Business Application Studio in an browser, he first gets redirected to the trusted identity provider (IDP) in the BTP subaccount to authenticate. In BTP, this task is delegated to an Identity Authentication Service (IAS) tenant of the SAP Cloud Identity Services using the OpenID Connect (OIDC) protocol. Since the IAS tenant is not aware of John's memberships in Entra security groups, it forwards the application's OIDC authorization request to Entra ID by acting as an OIDC proxy. This requires to configure the Entra ID tenant as a Corporate IDP in IAS which causes John to sign-in to Entra instead of IAS. Upon successful authentication, Entra sends the OIDC authentication response back to IAS, containing an authorization code to request the ID and access tokens from Entra.

To identify the IAS tenant as a legitimate client when sending the OIDC token request, Entra requires IAS to authenticate. OIDC defines several authentication methods for clients, such as a client secret, which is a shared secret with a configurable expiration date (maximum 24 months) generated by Entra and shared with IAS. The drawback of using a client secret is that it is sent every time when IAS performs client authentication to obtain the tokens from Entra. When the secret is about to reach its expiration, the secret must be rotated in Entra ID and shared again with the administrator of the IAS tenant.

To reduce the administrative overhead and avoid sending a shared secret, OIDC also supports client authentication based on an asymmetric key pair. Here, the IAS tenant as the OIDC (proxy) client generates a JSON Web Token (JWT) according to RFC 7532 and uses its private key to sign it. The corresponding public key certificate is shared with the Entra tenant to establish the trust relationship. Similar to the claim configuration of the issued tokens, trust to the IAS JWT signing certificate is configured in the SAP Cloud Identity Services Application Registration. 

Now that the information about John's membership to the Entra security group SAP BTP Developer can be securely transferred to BTP when John authenticates with IAS on BTP, one question still remains opens: How is John assigned to the Developer role in BTP if he is a member of the SAP BTP Developer group in Entra? Let's have a look at John's OAuth JWT access token issued by Entra ID in the token response to the IAS token request: 

Figure 2: Entra ID Access Token

John's membership to the SAP BTP Developer group is emitted as a claim with the name groups that contains an array of GUIDs (globally unique identifiers). Each GUID is the value of the Security group's object identifier (OID) attribute in Microsoft Entra. In BTP, a role collection can be assigned to a user group by mapping the group(s) OID(s) to the role collection. This approach follows the recommendations for federated authorization management between Entra and IAS, and will be configured in the following steps.

Prerequisites

To follow the steps in this tutorial, make sure you have access to the following services:

  • An IAS tenant with full administrative access. You can request an IAS trial instance as described here for your BTP trial account.
  • An SAP BTP subaccount with Space Manager access. A free trial account will work for this scenario. The IAS tenant is already configured as a trusted identity provider for the applications in this subaccount.
  • Administrator-level access to an Microsoft Entra ID P2 subscription. You can obtain a P2 tenant for development and learning purposes with a free Microsoft 365 E5 developer subscription. With this subscription you can request a trial of Microsoft Entra ID Governance by following these steps.
  • Two test users in your Entra ID tenant, one for requesting the access to BTP, and the other for approving it.

Register SAP Cloud Identity Services application in Entra

Let's get started with creating the application registration for SAP Cloud Identity Services and configuring the token claims for it.

StepDescriptionScreenshot
1

Login to the Entra admin center with your Entra administrator. Go to Identity -> Applications -> App Registrations.

Click New registration.

2

Enter a name, e.g. "SAP Cloud Identity Services".

Click Register.

3Copy the Application (client) ID from the Overview to the clipboard and paste it into a text file for later use.
4Click Endpoints.
5

Copy the OpenID Connect metadata document URL to the clipboard and paste it into a text file for later use.

Close the dialog window.

6

Select Token configuration from the menu.

Click Add groups claim.

7

Activate the Security groups checkbox.

Click Add.

OIDC corporate IDP configuration in IAS

The data captured from the app registration in Entra is now required to configure the OIDC proxy settings for the corporate IDP in IAS.

StepDescriptionScreenshot
8

Open a new tab in the browser and login to the Administration Console of your SAP CIS IAS tenant at  https://<ias-tenant-name>.accounts.ondemand.com/admin

Go to Identity Providers -> Corporate Identity Providers.

9Click Create.
10

Enter a Display Name, e.g. "BestRun Corp. Entra ID Tenant".

Select OpenID Connect Compliant for the Identity Provider Type.

Click Create.

11For the newly created Corportate Identity Provider, select OpenID Connect Configuration from the Trust tab.
12

Paste the URL copied in step 5 into the Discovery URL field.

Click Load.

13From the Client Authentication Method drop-down list, select Private key JWT.
14Paste the value of the Application (client) ID copied in step 3 into the Client ID field.
15Scroll down to the Scopes section and click Add.
16

Enter offline_access for the new scope value.

Click Save.

17

Scroll up and click Save.

Then, click OIDC Callback URL to copy the value into the clipboard.

18From the navigation menu, select Applications & Resources -> Tenant Settings
19On the Authentication tab, go to your tenant's OpenID Connect Configuration.
20Click the Download button of the default Signing Certificate.
21From the navigation menu, select Applications & Resources -> Applications
22

Select your SAP BTP subaccount listed under Bundled Applications.

Select Conditional Authentication from the SAP BTP subaccount's Trust settings.

23

From the Default Identity Provider drop-down list, select the corporate identity provider created earlier.

Click Save.

24Select Attributes from the SAP BTP subaccount's Trust settings.
25Click Add.
26

In the Name field of the new attribute, enter "groups".

Select "Corporate Identity Provider" as the Source.

Enter "${corporateIdP.groups}" in the Value field.

This additional attribute setting forwards the value of the groups claim in the Entra ID token to the BTP applications in the SAP BTP subaccount.

Click Save.

Complete the Application Registration in Entra

To complete the OIDC proxy setup between Entra ID and IAS, the trust relationship must be established by uploading the IAS signing certificate to Entra and registering the IAS callback URL.

StepDescriptionScreenshot
27

Go back to the Entra Admin center browser tab.

Go to Manage -> Authentication. 

Click Add Platform.

28Select Web.
29

Paste the OIDC Callback URL copied in step 17 into the Redirect URIs field.

Click Configure.

30

Go to Manage -> Certificates & Secrets.

On the Certificates tab, click Upload certificate.

31

Open the file upload dialog and select the certificate file you downloaded in step 20.

Click Add.

32The IAS signing cerificate is now listed under the Certificates tab.

Validate the OIDC Proxy Setup

Now that the OIDC proxy setup is completed in Entra, you can validate the configuration in IAS.

StepDescriptionScreenshot
33

Go back to the IAS Administration console browser tab.

Navigate to Identity Providers -> Corporate Identity Providers in the menu.

34

Select your corporate identity provider from the list and select OpenID Connect Configuration.

Click Validate.

35

Accept the requested permissions from the Entra SAP Cloud Identity Services app.

You may also activate the checkbox to consent on behalf-of your organization.

36You can now inspect the token sent by Entra to IAS and see for example the groups claim and its values for your user.

Create the user group in Entra

Continue the setup in the Entra Admin center by creating the group for SAP BTP Developers.

StepDescriptionScreenshot
37

Go back to the Entra Admin center browser tab.

Navigate to Identity -> Groups.

Click New Group.

38

Select Security for the Group type.

Enter a Group name, e.g. "SAP BTP Developer".

Select Assigned from the Membership type drop-down list.

Click Create.

39Select the newly created group from the list.
40Copy the Object ID to the clipboard and paste the value into a text file for later use.

Assign the group to the BTP role collection

Lorem ipsum

StepDescriptionScreenshot
41

In a new browser tab, login to your BTP subaccount in the BTP Cloud Cockpit. For a trial account, use this link.

Select your subaccount.

42

Navigate to Security -> Role collections.

Select the Business_Application_Studio_Developer role collection from the list.

Click Edit.

43Under User Groups, select the IAS tenant as the identity provider, and paste the Object ID from the Entra ID group you copied in step 40.

Create the Access Package

Now it is time to configure the workflow for requesting and approving access to BTP by creating the access package in Entra ID Governance entitlement management.

StepDescriptionScreenshot
44

Go back to the Entra Admin center browser tab.

Navigate to Identity Governance -> Entitlement management -> Access Packages.

Click New access package.

45

Enter a Name, e.g. "SAP BTP", and provide a short Description.

You can leave the assignment of the new access package to the General catalog.

Click Next: Resource roles.

46Click Groups and Teams.
47

Activate the checkbox to see all Groups and Teams not in the General catalog.

In the search field, enter the name of the group you created, e.g. "SAP BTP Developer"

Activate the checkbox of the group from the search results list.

Click Select.

48Click Next: Requests.
49Select For users in your directory and allow All members (excluding guests) therein to make a request for the access package.
50

Scroll down to the Approval section. 

Choose Yes to Require approval.

For the First Approver, select Choose specific approvers from the drop-down list.

Click Add approvers.

51Search for your user who will approve the access request and click Select.
52

Choose Yes to Enable new requests.

Click Next: Requestor information.

53Click Next: Lifecycle.
54Click Next: Rules.
55Click Next: Review + create.
56Click Create.
57Copy the link to MyAccess portal to the clipboard and paste it into a text file for later use.

Test access to BTP without the assignment

Now you are ready to test the scenario! Let's start with verifying that access is still denied for your test user.

StepDescriptionScreenshot
58

Go back to the BTP Cloud Cockpit browser tab.

Navigate to your subaccount's Services -> Instances and Subscriptions.

Copy the URL to the SAP Business Application Studio to the clipboard.

59

Open a new private browser window.

Paste the URL you copied in the previous step to your SAP Business Application Studio instance from the clipboard into the browser and hit Enter.

You are redirected by the IAS tenant to Entra ID as the corporate IDP for your BTP subaccount's business applications.

Login as the test user who will request access to SAP Business Application Studio.

60You see a message that the user has no access yet due to the missing assignment to the Developer role.

Request the access package

StepDescriptionScreenshot
61

Start the request workflow in a new tab of the private browser window by opening the URL to the MyAccess portal you copied in step 58.

Click Continue.

62Click Submit request.
63

You receive a confirmation message that your request to access the SAP BTP package is being processed.

Close the private browser window.

Approve the request

Now you take over the role as the approver.

StepDescriptionScreenshot
64

Re-open a private browser window. Start the approval workflow by opening the URL to the MyAccess portal you copied in step 57.

Login as the user who you selected in step 51 for approving requests of the SAP BTP access package.

65

Naviate to Approvals in the MyAccess portal.

Select your other test user's access request from the list of Pending approvals.

Click Approve.

66Click Submit.
67

You receive a confirmation message you approved the request.

Close the private browser window.

Test access with the assignment

Now let's try again to open SAP Business Application Studio as the test user with the approved access request.

StepDescriptionScreenshot
68

Re-open a private browser window. Go to the URL of the SAP Business Application Studio you copied in step 58.

You are redirected by the IAS tenant to Entra ID as the corporate IDP for your BTP subaccount's business applications.

Login as the test user who has requested access to SAP Business Application Studio.

69This time the user can successfully load and use the SAP Business Application Studio.

With this last step you've successfully completed the scenario and I hope you enjoyed it!

entry.title: SAP BTP Identity Services logon against SAP ECC or S4 backend?
entry.content:
Howdy!

So I have a requirement to setup an external facing WorkZone (Cloud Fiori Launchpad) which houses a few Fiori apps that connect to our SAP ECC EHP8 system (soon to be S4).  My requirement is that when a user access our WorkZone the logon page should authenticate against our SAP ECC system instead of the default BTP Open ID provider.

I've scoured the web and believe to have come across a few posts that seem to alude to a solution but even then I'm not sure.  For example, I've read some blogs/documents about activating the SAML 2.0 service on SAP and hooking that up to BTP identity services.  I've walked through these steps but it won't work; even then, it just doesn't feel like the right approach.  I've considered user propagation as a potential solution but haven't tried to implement it as I'm not even sure I'm headed in the right direction.

If anyone has any experience here I'd greatly appreciate some validation or success stories.  Much appreciated!

entry.title: Re: Devtoberfest 2024 Contest
entry.content: @Alexsander The points on the gameboard are real time based the badges you have in the Community.  However, some of the badges from tutorial completion are only loaded in nightly badges.  Then there are "manual" activities like loading all the Petoberfest entries.  We only load those badges once the special event day is complete.

entry.title: Re: Devtoberfest 2024 Week 1 Contest Activities
entry.content:
There was an issue with the just the ABAP Week 1 badge not getting assigned correctly.  The issue has been resolved and the missing badges loaded manually. 
I checked the profiles, and the following people do have this badge now:
@lin_fan_24 
@nik_sopra1234 
@Chiranjeevi1 
@Gus-Alvarez 
@kallesh_kc 
@nik_sopra1234 
@Sayontan_Das 

That's everyone that reported having an issue with this badge.  That makes me feel pretty sure that the issue is actually resolved, and everything caught up on this badge now.

entry.title: Re: Devtoberfest 2024 Week 1 Contest Activities
entry.content: @brahammittal If you have a technical question on a specific tutorial, you are probably best asking that in a separate thread and notifying the tutorial author via an @ ping.  Or consider using the Feeback button at the top of the tutorial.  There you can create an issue in GitHub that is automatically assigned to the tutorial author/maintainer.  Otherwise, it's likely to get lost in the general discussion around the contest that happens here in this thread.

entry.title: Teamplate for Variant configuration identifiers?
entry.content: Very good morning. I have good and basic knowledge about VC and its process. I have done minor implementations and have set up VC for certain ranges now based on role getting in to more challenging projects and need one help. Do we have any standard template which can be provided to users to fill in product details once they fill in it will help us to determine object dependencies and other required attributes to set up VC?

entry.title: Re: SAP EWM Stuck Queue - QM processing currently only possible for integrated warehouses
entry.content: I am having same issue in S4 2022

entry.title: Re: SAP Service Cloud - Version 2 - Utilities add on - Ho...
entry.content: To create a case linked to premise, we need to open the premise and than create the case right? If we open a case in the case object we can´t add a premise right?

entry.title: SAP Service Cloud - Version 2 - How to turn mandatory standard fields
entry.content:
Hello,

I would like to turn some standard fields mandatory as categories in cases,priority etc. and restrict fields edition based on rules. Is this possible in adaption mode? Or where i can do this rules? 

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Alternative to I_InventoryPriceByKeyDate
entry.content:
There is no relevant CDS view that can be used for development. After raising an Incident, I was told to raise a feature request which I did. Sadly the feature request got rejected saying I_InventoryPriceByKeyDate is already offered. Whoever rejected it obviously did not check that it cannot be used for backend development or in another CDS join.

I am not sure why is is not available as it is a genuine requirement to able able to see the historical cost of an item in a plant.

entry.title: Re: Block Calendar action in Required Learning configurat...
entry.content: There is a KBA from SAP indicating this is a bug and will be addressed in future release

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content: I do all this

entry.title: Re: More questions about downloading the CR Runtime 64bit...
entry.content:
Hi Rod,

Unfortunately those older SP's are no longer available due to security and other reasons.

And SAP only supports the latest SP which is currently SP36 which you can get from here also, and read all about the product:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SUPPORT_CONTENT/crystalreports/3354091173.html

I also wrote a blog on upgrading:

Upgrading a Visual Studio 2008->2022 .NET project with the latest CR .NET SDK packages

Note the end of the blog has info for WEB app's.

On your DEV PC install the EXE, note there is a 32 bit VS installer for VS 2010->2019 and a new installer for 64 bit bit VS 2022, you want the 32 bit version.

Also, don't install the MSI on your DEV PC, those are for installing on the App Server only, the EXE will install the 32 and 64 bit runtime.

I also suggest upgrading your Project to 64 bit, unless there are 32 bit dependencies that don't have a 64 bit version. Just set the IIS AppPool to 64 bit.

CR for Will soon no longer include 32 bit runtime, R&D and Product teams determined there is no reason for 32 bit anymore, same as it was when 16 bit applications ended life. 32 bit will so be a thing of the past. All CPU's are 64 capable and all Server OS' are all 64 bit now...

And there is no SP 6 available for download so not sure what you download.

Be sure to select Crystal Reports for Visual studio, CR installer no longer has the .NET runtime to install into VS.

Don

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP Identity Services logon against SAP ECC or S4 backend?
entry.content: Yes, you can do this. You will need to setup the cloud connector to use the Principal Type X.509 Certificate. Then on the the back end ABAP, setup icm trusted reverse proxy, with verifying the client cert enabled and certificate mapping rulebased enabled. After that, assumming that you have web dispatcher sitting infront of the ABAP instance, you have to also se up the icm forward cert hearder to forward the x.509 received from the cloud connector to your ABAP instance.

entry.title: Restricting Spot Award admin view for different admins depending on their verticals/department
entry.content:
Hi team,

We have multiple spotaward Programme for different verticals like (Agri, Minerals, Steel). The respective vertical admin needs to be given the admin permission to change the status (active/inactive) of these awards. But they shouldn't see other awards apart from thier own verticals.

How can we achieve this? Is it possible?

I tried to restrict it through RBP, but there it is asking to enter code. Can anyone help where to find the code of these respective spot award programme?

entry.title: Digital Skill Initiative
entry.content:
Hi @David_Chaviano 

My name is Roohi Sachan, and I am an IT professional with 5 years of experience. I identify with the following categories:

  • Woman
  • Individuals from remote or underserved locations who may not have access to the same economic opportunities as those in urban or more privileged areas
  • Minorities facing unequal opportunities in their geographic region
  • Mid-career professionals seeking to switch careers

I am excited about the SAP Learning Digital Skills Initiative, as I believe it could open doors to opportunities in SAP-related industries.

I would be honored to participate in this initiative, as it would bring me closer to achieving my goal of becoming an SAP consultant.

Thank you for considering my application for the free SAP certification.

entry.title: Re: Need help with last part of tutorial : Test yourself....not accepting only write statement.
entry.content:
i had the same problem like you, try to write the statement likes this:

out->write( 'Hello SAP Cloud Platform ABAP Environment!' ).

entry.title: Re: How to Customize the Flat File Contents During AO Upload - 0RSPL_FILE_UPLOAD_AO
entry.content: The parameters at the moment doesn't allow to pass error/validation messages

entry.title: SAP Lighthouse Program - Partner Use Cases adopting BTP GenAI Capabilities [Part 3]
entry.content:
Since 2023 the SAP office of the CTO has sponsored the SAP BTP Lighthouse Program with a mandate to  provide advanced access and hands-on support for BTP services with GenAI capabilities to partners and customers.

In the final part of this trilogy, I will highlight some of the amazing work that participating partners have created leveraging SAP BTP GenAI enabled services such as:

SAP Analytics Cloud Just ASK is a natural language query interface used to query data. Powered by artificial intelligence, Just Ask enables you to search your data easily and efficiently using business terms you are familiar with.

SAP Automation Pilot is primarily used to simplify and automate complex manual processes in order to minimize the operational effort behind any cloud solution in the SAP Business Technology Platform.

SAP Analytics Cloud Just ASK and SAP Automation Pilot were used by partners such as:

Sierra Digital to build solutions in Business Partner Onboarding, AP / AR Automation, Virtual Procurement Assistant, Plant Maintenance, Clean Core BTP Readiness Assessment etc. for customers such as NYS Housing & Community Renewal (NYSHCR), City of Houston and Energy Transfer Partners.

Check out part one of the series for partners that have adopted SAP HANA Cloud Vector Engine and SAP Generative AI Hub. Review part two of the series for partner that have adopted SAP Build Code. You can find out more about these partners and SAP BTP GenAI enabled services here.

Looking for even more adoption stories - there is exciting news coming up from the Lighthouse program partners and our product teams at SAP TechEd, Oct 8-9 2024, Register Today!

entry.title: Re: Internal Hiring: Recruiting-to-Employee Central
entry.content:
Hi @shrutithakkar Thank you for the fantastic blog! We have a business requirement to initiate crossboarding upon a legal entity change  from Recruiting, without terminating the employee record in Employee Central, as the business prefers not to use the legal entity transfer feature.
I have configured and tested legal entity change from recruiting (Legal entity change transfer event reason)  but it is bypassing Onboarding (Crossboarding) process however updating job info in EC with legal entity change as position transfer event reason not Legal entity change event reason.

Can you please clarify that crossboarding from Recruiting only supports Transfer of internal hire  within same legal entity/company on Transfer events ?

entry.title: Re: 🟡 Petoberfest 2024
entry.content: Eysan is absolutely adorable, taking over your workspace like a true boss! Looks like she's making sure everything is purrfect on the laptop.

entry.title: Change default tooltip to a shape in a Story
entry.content:
Hello - We have inserted a shape in one of our stories in SAC and this shape is linked to navigate to another story upon clicking on it. When we over the mouse, it shows as popup as shown in below image.

What is being shown in the tooltip is the technical name of the story. I could not find an option to change it. In the context menu I see only below options and I don't see any option in the styling too. Any inputs here would help.

Thanks,

PS

entry.title: Exit Fullscreen navigates to previous page in SAC Stories
entry.content:
Hello - Exit Fullscreen in SAC Story toolbar is navigating to the "Previous Webpage" most of the time instead of exiting the full screen and giving an option to navigate through the options such as Modeler, Data Actions.... This is happening in the quality system. "Exit Fullscreen" in Analytic Applications works perfectly though. Can you please guide?

Thanks,

PS

entry.title: can only create a project on Dev environment
entry.content:
I've assigned the same role in all the environment and I can only create a project on Dev environment. 

Is it normal? 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: can only create a project on Dev environment
entry.content:
Hi customer,

As of S/4HANA 2020, the system was classified to modifiable and non-modifiable systems. This is the reason why you can’t create a project in non-modifiable system as of S/4HANA 2020 but can do this in old product version by transaction code LTMC before S/4HANA 2020.

The reason and solution are documented in the following Note/KBA:

KBA 3115120 - Cannot create a new project when using Staging Tables method.

SAP Note 3104790 - Modifiable Systems and Transport Concept, SAP S/4HANA Migration Cockpit - Migrate Data Using Staging Tables.

Projects can only be created in systems that have the following settings:

  • Changes must be permitted for objects in the system at global level, cross client level, and client-specific level.
  • The development package must be assigned to a modifiable software component and a modifiable namespace (relevant as of SAP S/4HANA 2021)

Projects need to be created and configured in modifiable systems such as development systems and then be transferred using

  • b. the transport functionality in the migration object modeler (LTMOM) to non-modifiable systems as of release SAP S/4HANA 2021. Projects created in SAP S/4HANA 2020 can still use the export / import functionality in SAP S/4HANA 2021 FPS0 and FPS01. Please be aware, that we do not recommend importing projects to an SAP S/4HANA system with a different release.

More details are documented in the following slides. Please check page 10 to better understand this issue.

https://www.sap.com/documents/2021/10/6af5929d-007e-0010-bca6-c68f7e60039b.html

Best regards,
Ron
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: Posting journal entry line items with multi currencie...
entry.content: Hi Phoo, Only document currency is supported on the header of the accounting document. Your desired inputting different currencies by line is not supported. Kind Regards, Lucy

entry.title: how to turn off detailed scheduling for production order
entry.content:
Hi gurus,

we are incorporating a new company and need load some production orders(work in progress) from legacy sap. It is taking a long time scheduling order in CO01, so I'd like to know if its possible turn off detailed scheduling. We tried turn off through OPU3 and OPU7, but it is disabled. Is there a way to turn off or another place?

Thanks in advance.

Elaine

entry.title: S/4Hana Courses for beginer
entry.content:
I am working in Org where ECC/EC is implemented & soon they will be migrating to S/4 Hana. I can see many when i search but actually do not know where to start with. Can anyone suggest me the beginner courses for myself to get equipped with what is S/4 Hana and how i can educate myself morfe about what is it and its benefits

thx

entry.title: Employee Replication without PA_SA_IN
entry.content:
Hi,

We have a non-unicode erp system and PA_SA_IN addon can be deployed in unicode system only. Is it possible to customise the employee replication without using PA_SA_IN addon? 

What services would we have to build and the Infotypes involved?

What are the options apart from converting system to unicode.

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP Identity Services logon against SAP ECC or S4 backend?
entry.content: I don't think this is what OP wants, he wants the ecc to be user store during the authentication process, not sso to ecc using principal propagation.

entry.title: Re: Report(s) that show After the Fact (ATF) Purchase Orders
entry.content: Hi Marcus, Please help to clarify what's your purchase order date mean? For the purchasing order, I would like to explain it first, based on the sort key setting with purchase order no. in the GR/IR account, you will get the purchasing order no. in the line item level after posting the invoice verification. Kind Regards, Lucy

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP Identity Services logon against SAP ECC or S4...
entry.content:
I would try to stay away from using back end ecc user store for authentication and use x.509 SSO whenever possible. Users on the backend ECC still have to have proper authorizations in order for apps to work.

I would focus on letting Identity Provider handling the authentication work and ECC only have to worry about the apps and data once it gets past the authentication.

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP Identity Services logon against SAP ECC or S4...
entry.content: By the way, Elion, it was proven to work fine
